claim.observation: Recursive fls (full paths, offset 0) shows a DELETED directory subtree under C:\Windows\Logs\WindowsServerBackup\7.15\research\Rare-Earth Elements\ named 'M&A Targets' (inode 124754-144-1), containing subdirectories 'Orocobre' (124755) and 'Tronox' (124779) with PDF documents (e.g., TRONOX_2015.pdf, inode 124789). A deleted sibling file 'M&A Targets.zip' (inode 124753-128-1) sits in the same 'Rare-Earth Elements' directory. PDFs in the tree carry ':Zone.Identifier' alternate data streams (e.g., on 16Jan18_OREInvestorPresentation.pdf).
claim.interpretation: A collection of external research on rare-earth-element companies was assembled on this file server and then deleted. The directory name 'M&A Targets' and its contents (annual reports, investor presentations, technical reports for the named companies) indicate gathered merger-and-acquisition research. The ':Zone.Identifier' alternate data streams indicate the PDFs were downloaded from another security zone (typically the internet). The subtree sits under a path that mimics the Windows Server Backup directory rather than a normal user document area, and the deleted 'M&A Targets.zip' (related finding on inode 124753) is co-located with it. This is consistent with aggregation of sensitive business research into one tree that was then compressed and removed. It does not by itself establish who created it, nor that the data was transferred off the host.
